text: I'll ingest this meeting into your wiki. Let me start by reading the config and scanning existing wiki pages.

content:
1	# Second Brain Wiki Configuration
2	
3	This file configures the skill for Evan's environment. Edit paths and
4	projects as your work evolves.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Paths
9	
10	| Path | Value | Description |
11	|------|-------|-------------|
12	| Wiki directory | `~/second-brain/wiki/` | Where wiki pages live |
13	| Sources directory | `~/second-brain/sources/` | Raw source files stored here |
14	| Inbox directory | `~/Desktop/Second Brain Inbox/` | Drop files here for processing |
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Active Projects
19	
20	These are your current projects and areas of focus. The skill uses these
21	to find connections when ingesting new content.
22	
23	- **evaluator-super-agent** — automated prototype quality assessment using persona-driven walkthroughs, 7-dimension rubric scoring, actor/evaluator separation
24	- **prototype-creator** — generates clickable prototypes from RFEs, tiered AC classification, Playwright automation
25	- **agent-eval-harness** — framework for evaluating AI skill quality, eval.yaml configs, judges, scoring pipelines
26	- **ai-sdlc-pipeline** — three-phase pipeline: RFE Creator (WHAT) → Prototype Creator (SHOW) → Strat Creator (HOW)
27	- **my-cursor-claw** — personal workspace with 20+ skills, MCP connections, notetaking, automations
28	- **decision-kit** — Decision Driven Development toolkit, /strategize, /decide, /journal
29	- **automated-usability-testing** — simulated usability testing tool, persona YAML files, evaluate-flow protocol
30	- **archie** — UX research agent for the RHOAIUX team
31	- **rhoai-prototypes** — RHOAI prototype collection for evaluation
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Calendar Integration
36	
37	| Setting | Value |
38	|---------|-------|
39	| Calendar name | `primary` |
40	| Auto-ingest meetings | `true` |
41	| Minimum meeting duration | `15 min` |
42	| Require notes/transcript | `true` |
43	| Skip recurring without notes | `true` |
44	
45	Events that qualify for auto-ingestion:
46	- Have attached meeting notes or a description with substance (not just "Weekly sync")
47	- Have a linked Google Doc (agenda, transcript, action items)
48	- Duration is 15+ minutes
49	- Are not recurring meetings that had no notes added
50	
51	---
52	
53	## MCP Integrations
54	
55	| Integration | MCP Server | Used for |
56	|-------------|-----------|----------|
57	| Google Docs | `user-google-workspace` | Extracting doc content |
58	| Google Calendar | `user-google-workspace` | Meeting ingestion |
59	| Slack | `project-0-my-cursor-claw-slack` | Channel message ingestion |
60	
61	---
62	
63	## Cross-Link Priority
64	
65	When multiple connections exist, prioritize these categories (from the
66	wiki index):
67	
68	1. **Red Hat AI-SDLC** — pipeline components, RFE/prototype/strat flow
69	2. **Evaluation & Quality** — rubrics, heuristics, eval harness
70	3. **Tools & Skills** — decision kit, cursor claw, skill overlays
71	4. **Meetings** — meeting notes with action items and decisions
72	5. **Standups** — sprint progress and blockers
73	6. **Session Logs** — what was built and shipped
74

1	# Wiki Page Template
2	
3	Use this template when creating new wiki pages. Copy the structure,
4	fill in the bracketed fields.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Template
9	
10	```markdown
11	# <Page Title>
12	
13	<One-sentence summary of what this page covers.>
14	
15	## Key Points
16	
17	- <Bullet point 1: a concrete fact, concept, or takeaway>
18	- <Bullet point 2>
19	- <Bullet point 3>
20	- <Additional bullets as needed, max 10>
21	
22	## Related
23	
24	- [<Related Page Title>](<related-slug>.md) — <one-line reason for connection>
25	- [<Another Related Page>](<slug>.md) — <connection reason>
26	
27	## Sources
28	
29	- <path/to/source/file or URL where this came from>
30	- Ingested: <YYYY-MM-DD>
31	```
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Good Example
36	
37	```markdown
38	# Retrieval Augmented Generation
39	
40	Technique that grounds LLM responses in retrieved documents rather than
41	relying solely on training data.
42	
43	## Key Points
44	
45	- Combines a retriever (vector search) with a generator (LLM)
46	- Reduces hallucination by providing source documents as context
47	- Trade-off: retrieval quality limits generation quality
48	- Common stack: embeddings → vector DB → reranker → LLM prompt
49	- Works best when source corpus is well-chunked and up-to-date
50	
51	## Related
52	
53	- [Vector Databases](vector-databases.md) — the retrieval layer in RAG pipelines
54	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — uses embedding similarity for judge calibration
55	
56	## Sources
57	
58	- ~/second-brain/sources/what-is-rag.md
59	- Ingested: 2026-06-10
60	```
61	
62	---
63	
64	## Bad Example
65	
66	```markdown
67	# RAG
68	
69	Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique in artificial intelligence
70	that combines information retrieval with text generation. It was first proposed
71	by Lewis et al. in 2020. The basic idea is that instead of relying solely on
72	what a language model learned during training, you can augment it with relevant
73	documents retrieved at inference time.
74	
75	This approach has several advantages. First, it reduces hallucination because
76	the model can reference actual documents. Second, it allows the model to access
77	up-to-date information that wasn't in its training data. Third, it provides
78	transparency because you can trace responses back to source documents.
79	
80	There are several implementations of RAG available today...
81	```
82	
83	**Why it's bad:**
84	- Reads like a Wikipedia article, not a wiki node
85	- No bullet summaries (walls of prose)
86	- No Related section (isolated page, no connections)
87	- No Sources section (where did this come from?)
88	- Title is an acronym without expansion
89	- Contains filler ("There are several..." "This approach has...")
90

content:
=== evaluator-super-agent ===
# Evaluator Super Agent

Evan's primary assignment: build an evaluation agent that automates prototype quality assessment within the [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) pipeline.

**Status: Shipped.** The evaluator is a 3-phase skill pipeline on branch `evan/prototype-evaluate` at [andybraren/prototype-creator](https://github.com/andybraren/prototype-creator). It has evaluated 18+ STRATs against Jira acceptance criteria with Playwright persona walkthroughs.

## What It Does (current)

| Capability | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|

=== agent-eval-harness ===
# Agent Eval Harness

A framework for evaluating AI agent skill quality through automated testing, judge scoring, and iterative optimization.

## How It Works

1. **eval.yaml** — defines execution mode, dataset schema, output descriptions, judges, models, and thresholds
2. **Dataset** — test cases with realistic inputs for the skill
3. **Judges** — automated scorers that rate skill outputs against criteria
4. **Run** — executes the skill against test cases, scores with judges, reports results

=== nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge ===
# Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge

First hands-on internship assignment: apply Nielsen Norman's 10 Usability Heuristics to Kubernetes-style interfaces across multiple AI tools and compare their analysis quality.

## The Jagged Edge

Research from Harvard Business School shows AI capabilities aren't uniform — models are very good at some tasks and surprisingly bad at others. The boundary between "good" and "bad" isn't obvious. This is the "jagged technological frontier."

## What Was Tested


=== ux-quality-rubric ===
# UX Quality Rubric

The scoring system used by [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) to evaluate generated prototypes. Scored by a restricted `prototype-scorer` agent (no shell, no network, no MCP).

## Dimensions (0–2 each, 8 total)

| Dimension | What It Measures |
|---|---|
| **Completeness** | Does the prototype cover the RFE's user stories and acceptance criteria? |
| **Usability** | Is the interaction pattern clear and free of obvious friction? (Nielsen's 10 heuristics) |

=== prototype-creator ===
# Prototype Creator

Phase 2 ("the SHOW") of Red Hat's [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md). Takes RFE user stories and produces rapid prototypes that make the proposed experience tangible before engineering commits.

## What It Does

Given an RFE from the [RFE Creator](rfe-creator.md) pipeline or Jira directly, this pipeline:

1. **Create** — Generates a prototype from RFE user stories at a specified fidelity level
2. **Review** — Scores against a 4-dimension [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md)

=== andy-evan-2026-06-29 ===
# Andy/Evan Meeting -- June 29, 2026

1:1 covering prototype evaluator progress, reporting infrastructure, repository strategy, and future directions.

## Key Discussion Points

- **Persona walkthroughs separated:** Fixed overlap issue. Developing "Findings" tab documenting issues and resolutions. Implementing "Auto-fix" vs "Report-only" modes to manage regressions.
- **Data discrepancies:** Patient scores fluctuating without clear explanation. Investigating inconsistencies.
- **Jira entity confusion:** Clarified RF (Product Managers create) → Strat relationship. Prototype Creator should ideally link to RF (upstream). "UI Direction" section in Strat documents remains useful for prototyping.
- **Token optimization:** Configure AI to extract only necessary sections (e.g., "UI Direction") rather than ingesting entire Strat documents. Need to confirm if it's a hardcoded heading.

# Wiki Index

A catalog of all wiki pages, organized by category.

## Red Hat AI-SDLC

- [Red Hat AI-First Lessons](redhat-ai-first-lessons.md) — Jeremy Eder's presentation on agentic SDLC, issue-to-PR bots, eval infrastructure, AgentReady, and culture shifts
- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) — proposal to formalize outcomes as the upstream step feeding the entire pipeline
- [AI-SDLC Pipeline](ai-sdlc-pipeline.md) — the three-phase pipeline (WHAT → SHOW → HOW)
- [RFE Creator](rfe-creator.md) — Phase 1: creates and assesses RFEs in Jira
- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — Phase 2: generates clickable prototypes from RFEs
- [Prototype Creator Demo — May 14](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md) — full walkthrough of automated + human-in-the-loop workflows (Andy, Beau, Dash)
- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — 4-dimension scoring system for prototypes

## Machine Learning & AI

- [Vector Similarity Search](vector-similarity-search.md) — ANN algorithms (HNSW, IVF) for finding semantically similar content via embeddings
- [Vector Databases](vector-databases.md) — specialized stores for embedding-based nearest-neighbor search
- [Retrieval-Augmented Generation](retrieval-augmented-generation.md) — grounds LLM responses in retrieved documents via vector search

## Evaluation & Quality

- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — automated prototype quality assessment (in progress)
- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — framework for evaluating AI skill quality
- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — AI model comparison using usability heuristics

## Tools & Skills

- [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) — Decision Driven Development toolkit
- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) — personal workspace with 20+ skills, 5 MCP connections, and automated daily review
---
- Created: andy-evan-eval-review-2026-06-16.md — Outcomes grounding, super evaluator architecture, overlay approach, hint mechanism, screenshot consolidation

73:## Related
## Related

- [Outcome Tiger Team](outcome-tiger-team.md) — proposes outcome-level awareness for evaluation, directly extending this agent's scope
- [Prototype Creator Demo — May 14](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md) — walkthrough with design insights for this agent
- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — the pipeline this plugs into
- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — existing scoring to extend
- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — pattern for automated eval loops
- [Decision Kit](decision-kit.md) — using /strategize to plan this agent
- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — informs methodology choice
- [My Cursor Claw](my-cursor-claw.md) — the workspace where this will be built
- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — heuristic eval pilot connects to automated evaluation vision

## Updates from June 16, 2026 Review

Decisions made with Andy Braren (see [Andy/Evan Eval Review — June 16](andy-evan-eval-review-2026-06-16.md)):

- **Architecture confirmed:** Centralized super evaluator, not fragmented skills. One Playwright pass, shared screenshots.
- **Grounding confirmed:** Outcomes as primary criteria, Strategic Goals excluded for now.
- **Overlay approach adopted:** 98% generic + 2% product-specific layer. See [GitLab skill-overlays best practice](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/global-engineering/wg-agentic-sdlc/-/blob/main/best-practices/skill-overlays.md).
- **Hint mechanism shipped:** Simulated UX researcher provides next URL on failure, allowing evaluation to continue while logging failure impact.
- **Screenshot consolidation:** Sub-agents share one set of Playwright screenshots instead of each capturing independently.

- [RH AI-First UX — 2026-06-18](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-18.md) — Evan demoed automated usability testing + PatternFly consistency checker to full team
- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 22](andy-evan-2026-06-22.md) — eval iteration history, flagging system, Prototype Creator integration strategy
- [AI-First Sync — June 25](ai-first-sync-2026-06-25.md) — Evan's full evaluator presentation to the team, readiness check discussion
- [RH AI-First UX — June 25](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-25.md) — testing fragmentation concerns, unified testing proposal
- [Standup — June 29](standup-2026-06-29.md) — 2 automated usability testing agents in development
- [Skill Overlays](skill-overlays.md) — context engineering technique relevant to team-specific overlays
- [Andy/Evan Meeting — June 29](andy-evan-2026-06-29.md) — findings tab, auto-fix vs report-only modes, X-ray vs blind evaluation, centralized dashboard
- [Midpoint Check-in — June 30](midpoint-checkin-2026-06-30.md) — Zack: "pioneering work" combining research + AI + SDLC; report must be immediately actionable for designers

## Sources

1	# Evaluator Super Agent
2	
3	Evan's primary assignment: build an evaluation agent that automates prototype quality assessment within the [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) pipeline.
4	
5	**Status: Shipped.** The evaluator is a 3-phase skill pipeline on branch `evan/prototype-evaluate` at [andybraren/prototype-creator](https://github.com/andybraren/prototype-creator). It has evaluated 18+ STRATs against Jira acceptance criteria with Playwright persona walkthroughs.
6	
7	## What It Does (current)
8	
9	| Capability | Source | Status |
10	|---|---|---|
11	| AC evaluation | Custom Playwright persona journeys against Jira criteria | Shipped |
12	| Usability scoring | 7-dimension rubric with persona overlays (Zack Bodnar's framework) | Shipped |
13	| Consistency checking | Beau Morley's consistency checker (source + visual mode) | Shipped |
14	| Navigation reachability | Playwright verifies pages are clickable from sidebar | Shipped |
15	| Human review | Review tab in HTML report with override buttons + CSV writeback | Shipped |
16	| Report hosting | GitLab Pages (eval-reports-7ebc0e.pages.redhat.com) | Shipped |
17	| Google Sheet sync | Automated pass/fail + usability metrics to tracking sheet | Shipped |
18	| Feedback loop | Eval results → prototype-refine → re-eval (max 3 iterations) | In progress |
19	
20	## Architecture (confirmed June 16 with Andy)
21	
22	Evolved from "single monolithic super agent" to a **phased pipeline with overlays**:
23	
24	```
25	Phase 1: prototype-evaluate (extract ACs, RFE, Outcome, personas, journeys)
26	    ↓ writes extract-state.json
27	Phase 2: evaluate-journey (Playwright walkthroughs, consistency check)
28	    ↓ writes journey-log.json, screenshots, CSV
29	Phase 3: evaluate-usability (7-dimension persona scoring)
30	    ↓ updates journey-log.json
31	Phase 4: render-report.js + sync-sheet.js (HTML, CSV, Google Sheet)
32	```
33	
34	**Key design decisions:**
35	1. **Outcomes over Strategic Goals** as grounding criteria (Outcomes are consistently written)
36	2. **One Playwright pass** — shared screenshots across usability, consistency, and AC checks
37	3. **98% generic + 2% product overlay** (`config/product-overlay.yaml`) — portable to other products
38	4. **Hint mechanism** for dead-end navigation (failure logged but journey continues)
39	5. **Human-in-the-loop** — Review tab for designer input before feedback loop acts
40	
41	## Research References
42	
43	- **Avenir-UX** — autonomous UX evaluation agent using SEQ/SUS + persona framework
44	- **UXCascade** — multi-agent persona simulation with severity-rated issues (S1–S4)
45	- GitLab Notes REST API for MR comment posting
46	- Danger bot framework for CI-integrated review automation
47	
48	## How It Connects
49	
50	```
51	Jira STRAT (Outcome → RFE → STRAT)
52	    → prototype-create (generates code in workspace)
53	    → prototype-evaluate (this agent — 3 phases)
54	        ├── Phase 1: Extract ACs, personas, journeys from Jira
55	        ├── Phase 2: Playwright journeys + consistency check
56	        ├── Phase 3: Usability dimension scoring
57	        └── Phase 4: Report + Sheet sync
58	    → Human reviews report (override verdicts, add notes)
59	    → prototype-refine (acts on suggestions + human annotations)
60	    → Re-evaluate (loop until PASS or max iterations)
61	    → prototype-submit (MR to GitLab)
62	```
63	
64	## Key Design Inputs from Demo (May 14)
65	
66	From the [Prototype Creator Demo](prototype-creator-demo-may14.md):
67	- Consistency checkers should only evaluate what's NEW (CSS class="new" tagging)
68	- Even auto-mode should produce decision-kit pages for post-review
69	- Evaluator needs outcome-level awareness, not just feature-level
70	- Preview URL is the evaluation entry point (load the MR URL, assess in browser context)
71	- Token efficiency matters — scope evaluation to changed areas only
72

84

90	- **Grounding confirmed:** Outcomes as primary criteria, Strategic Goals excluded for now.
91	- **Overlay approach adopted:** 98% generic + 2% product-specific layer. See [GitLab skill-overlays best practice](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/global-engineering/wg-agentic-sdlc/-/blob/main/best-practices/skill-overlays.md).
92	- **Hint mechanism shipped:** Simulated UX researcher provides next URL on failure, allowing evaluation to continue while logging failure impact.
93	- **Screenshot consolidation:** Sub-agents share one set of Playwright screenshots instead of each capturing independently.

1	# Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge
2	
3	First hands-on internship assignment: apply Nielsen Norman's 10 Usability Heuristics to Kubernetes-style interfaces across multiple AI tools and compare their analysis quality.
4	
5	## The Jagged Edge
6	
7	Research from Harvard Business School shows AI capabilities aren't uniform — models are very good at some tasks and surprisingly bad at others. The boundary between "good" and "bad" isn't obvious. This is the "jagged technological frontier."
8	
9	## What Was Tested
10	
11	- Selected Kubernetes/OpenShift UI screens at different complexity levels
12	- Crafted evaluation prompts asking each tool to assess against all 10 heuristics with severity ratings (1–4)
13	- Ran the same prompt through Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini (Flash + Pro), and ChatGPT
14	
15	## Comparison Dimensions
16	
17	- Quality of analysis — did it correctly identify real usability issues?
18	- Depth — surface-level observations vs. genuine insight?
19	- Specificity — generic advice vs. grounded in the actual interface?
20	- Structure — how well-organized and actionable?
21	- Hallucinations — did it make things up?
22	- Unique insights — did any tool catch something others missed?
23	
24	## Deliverables
25	
26	- [Jaquez - Task 1 - Nielsen & Agents](https://docs.google.com/document/d/11o9Px8wddeal3Ap7vBVtyDSw8FWaMGsuTYQwyc0hSAQ) (Google Doc)
27	- [Update — Evan Jaquez](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1AoUZtCCJf4btr68jOY3_xKbJU2wyx6XTqjGn6IIRd3w) (Google Slides)
28	
29	## Relevance to Current Work
30	
31	The comparison methodology directly informs the [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — understanding which AI tools produce research-quality heuristic evaluations vs. shallow summaries is critical for choosing the right approach to automated prototype evaluation.
32	
33	## Related
34	
35	- [UX Quality Rubric](ux-quality-rubric.md) — rubric-based scoring approach
36	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — applies these learnings
37	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — where evaluations will be used
38	- [Ansible AI Helpers Strategy — 2026-06-17](ansible-ai-helpers-strategy-2026-06-17.md) — heuristic eval skill selected as Ansible pilot
39	- [Standup — June 29](standup-2026-06-29.md) — usability comparison approach being iterated on
40	- [RH AI-First UX — June 25](rh-ai-first-ux-2026-06-25.md) — unified testing strategy connects to heuristic methodology
41	
42	## Sources
43	
44	- sources/2026-06-03-nielsen-agents-task1.html

1	# UX Quality Rubric
2	
3	The scoring system used by [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) to evaluate generated prototypes. Scored by a restricted `prototype-scorer` agent (no shell, no network, no MCP).
4	
5	## Dimensions (0–2 each, 8 total)
6	
7	| Dimension | What It Measures |
8	|---|---|
9	| **Completeness** | Does the prototype cover the RFE's user stories and acceptance criteria? |
10	| **Usability** | Is the interaction pattern clear and free of obvious friction? (Nielsen's 10 heuristics) |
11	| **Feasibility** | Can this be built with the target design system (PatternFly 6)? |
12	| **Fidelity Match** | Does the prototype match the requested fidelity level — not over or under-engineered? |
13	
14	## Pass Threshold
15	
16	6+ total with no zeros. Prototypes scoring above get `prototype-creator-rubric-pass`. Below get `prototype-creator-needs-attention`.
17	
18	## Scoring Agent
19	
20	One invocation per dimension ensures scores are deterministic and auditable. The scorer agent is restricted — no shell, no network, no MCP — to prevent contamination.
21	
22	## Connection to Evaluator Super Agent
23	
24	The [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) will extend this rubric with additional dimensions from simulated usability and desirability testing. See also [Nielsen's Heuristics](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) for the research that informs the usability dimension.
25	
26	## Related
27	
28	- [Prototype Creator](prototype-creator.md) — the pipeline that uses this rubric
29	- [Evaluator Super Agent](evaluator-super-agent.md) — extends this scoring
30	- [Nielsen's Heuristics & The Jagged Edge](nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md) — informs the usability dimension
31	- [Agent Eval Harness](agent-eval-harness.md) — similar pattern of rubric-based automated scoring
32	
33	## Sources
34	
35	- sources/2026-06-09-prototype-creator-overview.html
36	- andybraren/prototype-creator config/ux-rubric.yaml

text:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Created:** `weekly-design-sync-2026-07-01.md`

**Cross-linked with 3 existing pages:**
- `evaluator-super-agent.md` — Evan's demo next week is the direct action item from this meeting
- `nielsens-heuristics-jagged-edge.md` — team formally adopted it as the base rubric
- `ux-quality-rubric.md` — scoring dimensions flagged for revisit at the next meeting

**Active project connections:** This meeting touches three of your active projects — `evaluator-super-agent` (demo prep), `automated-usability-testing` (Zack's question on automation progress), and `prototype-creator` (evaluation results discussed from last sprint).

**Suggested reads:** You might want to revisit [Midpoint Check-in — June 30](midpoint-checkin-2026-06-30.md) before the demo — Zack's feedback there ("report must be immediately actionable for designers") is directly relevant to how you frame the evaluator demo next week.
